meaning: n.腋下,二腕的内侧vt.挽住...手臂
examples: The gimp came again with a roll of old newspapers under his oxter.
Normal style: exposure suit is designed with a sharp-angled or ring neckline, collocated with gallus trousers or apron, with free breath holes covering back and oxter; or dust coat.
The arms can get more energy this way and it is good for the lymphatic tissue in the oxter. In addition, raising arms can get through and open many meridians and bring the good effect.
